Patch Set 2:

> I'm not sure I feel like the code looks better with this patch, but i'm not against it

What would you think if it was change to

 while ((xxx = list_first_entry_or_null(head, struct foo, field)) != NULL) {

??
Conceptually I like that more, but the 'while' line get a little long.

Patch Set 2:

I don't feel like it makes a big difference.

For me (but, that's just me), a construct like:

   for_each_entry {
       list_del
       free
   }

are clear on its intent.
I will let other review that.

I do like you suggestion Neil. Let me update.

parents.subject: LU-14385 tests: verify lfs setstripe comp-flags and flags options

commit.subject: LU-9859 tracefile: convert list_for_each_entry_safe() to while(!list_empty())
commit.message:
LU-9859 tracefile: convert list_for_each_entry_safe() to while(!list_empty())

These loops are removing all elements from a list.
So using while(!list_empty()) makes the intent clearer.

Linux-commit: fdafb01e2c70e6b5321d158a2ff1f20a13d9b365

Change-Id: Idda25888e424a1deaa4d7c6fad427d494b1f56e5
Signed-off-by: NeilBrown <neilb@suse.de>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/41670
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Yang Sheng <ys@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
